goal: 用 SQLite 做 intents 查询缓存

summary.title: SQLite intent query cache
summary.what: Added a derived SQLite cache at .ml-cache/views/mainline.db that is rebuilt whenever the MainlineView JSON cache is written. The cache stores log-oriented scalar intent fields plus lookup tables for files, tags, subsystems, decisions, risks, and anti-patterns. mainline log now reads from the SQLite cache when available and falls back to the existing JSON view if the cache is missing or unreadable. Added focused storage tests for cache rebuild/query behavior and lookup-table population.
summary.why: The existing JSON view remains useful for compatibility and inspection, but it forces commands to read and unmarshal the full materialized view even for simple queries. A local SQLite cache lets Mainline keep git actor refs and notes as the source of truth while giving high-traffic read paths a structured index that can scale beyond the current dogfood-size intents.json.
summary.user_goal: 用 SQLite 做 intents 查询缓存

decisions.chose: Use SQLite as a derived .ml-cache query cache, not as the source of truth
decisions.rationale: Mainline's durable truth remains git actor logs plus notes; keeping JSON view as compatibility fallback preserves recovery and inspectability while allowing indexed reads.
The pure-Go driver avoids cgo distribution problems for a CLI. Version v1.34.5 keeps the module compatible with the repo's go 1.22 directive.

decisions.chose: Move mainline log to SQLite first with JSON fallback
decisions.rationale: log is a small, visible read path that benefits from precomputed activity_at and scalar fields without requiring a broader context retrieval rewrite.
A broken local cache should not make sync/view writes fail; callers can continue through the existing JSON fallback.

rejected.reason: That would remove the simple JSON compatibility/debug surface and make local cache corruption more disruptive.

rejected.alternative: Add FTS or move all context retrieval to SQLite immediately
rejected.reason: The first step only needs structured indexed tables and one migrated read path; FTS/context can build on the schema after this cache proves stable.
It would introduce cgo, making cross-platform CLI installation and distribution harder.

risks: The pure-Go SQLite driver adds a larger transitive dependency graph than the previous JSON-only storage path, which future dependency and binary-size reviews should watch.

The SQLite cache rebuild is best-effort; if cache writes silently fail, log remains correct through JSON fallback but does not get the intended indexed-read performance until the cache is rebuilt successfully.
followups: Use the new files/tags/subsystems tables to narrow context --files candidates without full JSON-view scans.
Evaluate FTS5 for context --query after measuring deterministic keyword retrieval on larger intent sets.
Consider surfacing cache rebuild failures as a status diagnostic instead of silently relying on fallback.

architectural_claims: SQLite is introduced as a derived local cache under .ml-cache/views/mainline.db, rebuilt from MainlineView writes.
The JSON MainlineView remains the compatibility fallback for log and the broader read surface.
The SQLite schema separates intent scalar fields from files, tags, subsystems, decisions, risks, and anti-pattern lookup tables.
behavioral_changes: mainline log uses Store.ReadIndexedLogIntents when the SQLite cache exists and falls back to Store.ReadMainlineView otherwise.
Store.WriteMainlineView now attempts to rebuild the SQLite cache after successfully writing .ml-cache/views/mainline.json.
Log activity_at for cached rows is precomputed during cache rebuild, avoiding per-log recomputation for cached view rows.

data_model_changes.name: .ml-cache/views/mainline.db
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Derived local cache; rebuilt from MainlineView and safe to delete.

security_implications: No new network or credential surface; the database stores the same local intent metadata already present in .ml-cache/views/mainline.json.
migration_notes: Existing repos continue to work without the SQLite cache because log falls back to mainline.json.
The cache is generated on the next MainlineView write, typically sync or any code path calling WriteMainlineView.
